query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
I've completed the research and submitted the structured findings.

**Bottom line: No — Uganda's Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) case count has NOT risen above 20.** It remains at **20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths**, with the last confirmed case dated **21 June 2026**. No new confirmed cases have been reported after that date through the latest updates (ECDC outbreak page updated 10 July 2026; WHO DON612; ECDC CDTR Week 27). Uganda also has 1 probable case (deceased) and 16 recoveries. This contrasts sharply with the DRC arm of the outbreak, which surged to ~1,792 confirmed cases and 625 deaths by 8–9 July 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (WHO Disease Outbreak News DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from DRC, 20 from Uganda and one from France; and 454 deaths including two from Uganda. Uganda's last confirmed case was 21 June 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda (ECDC outbreak page)
sources.snippet: As of 9-10 July 2026: Uganda 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths, 16 recovered; no new cases reported since 21 June 2026.

sources.title: Communicable disease threats report, 27 June - 3 July 2026, Week 27 (ECDC CDTR)
sources.snippet: Weekly CDTR covering 27 June - 3 July 2026 includes an Ebola update; consistent with Uganda holding at 20 confirmed cases.

sources.title: Disease Outbreak News: Ebola Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (3 July 2026)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, as well as one probable case who has died.

sources.title: Bundibugyo virus disease updates - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 8 July 2026, health authorities reported 1792 laboratory-confirmed cases in DRC; Uganda deaths at two.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: 625 confirmed deaths in DRC and two deaths in Uganda as of the second week of July 2026; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared 16 May 2026.

data_points.metric: Total confirmed cases (all locations) per WHO DON612
